# Gemini Code Assist \- Installation Guide

## Introduction

The objective of this document is to help install Gemini Code Assist plugins/extensions in coding IDEs and configure them for correct usage.  
	Supported IDEs (**installation was tested in the latest version**):

* Gemini CLI  
* Android Studio  
* IntelliJ/PyCharm  
* VSCode

	Additional resources:

* [Gemini Code Assist \- Security Policies and Best Practices](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1kdRPKtnE9K28ryiv0_hlFNWOPUmIaATds7rL3duY6-M/edit?usp=sharing)  
* For China team please use [Chinese guide](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1iuaWcnVpudD2MW7OZPH3f2c9OBI1Iv2LuvFYjMmyzX4/edit?tab=t.0) to setup proxy and access Google  
* [Motorola MCP Server \- Configuration Guide](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1B0wyZBLgh819eYDtpV2CssybgPc7gpOB8Q2riUOiohE/edit?usp=sharing)  
* [Gemini Code Assist \- Gemini CLI Extensions](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ht-0bIFOeXunz4C3OyWu25lTeLfbHBT6GhWuvcmVQas/edit?usp=sharing)  
* [Gemini Code Assist Knowledge Sharing & Office Hours](https://lenovo-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/p/luizfrf/IgBo4RDfwm-vQoFTZfmep6zjAXU3zEW1YVlG63QEKyNsndU)

## **Mandatory (Gemini CLI Installation)**

	**Google updated the IDEs and now Gemini CLI installation is mandatory to collect logs and use Agent Mode in some IDEs. It is mandatory to install and correctly configure Gemini CLI.**

1. Install [NodeJS](https://nodejs.org/en/download) (version 22 or higher) if you do not have it  
   1. If you want to run Gemini CLI in a build server, use the Node installation from the apps toolchain

```shell
(build server) export PATH=/apps/android/node-v22.12.0-linux-x64/bin:$PATH
(build server) export PATH=/localrepo/$USER/bin:$PATH
```

   2. If you do not want to run these commands every time, put these export commands at the end of your **/home/\<user\>/.bashrc**  
2. Install Gemini CLI with NPM (in your local user):  
   1. Local machine:

```shell
(local machine) npm install -g @google/gemini-cli
```

   2. Build server:

```shell
(build server) npm install -g --prefix /localrepo/$USER @google/gemini-cli
```

	\-Note  
To do steps 1 & 2 automatically everytime you login to the build server, put the following in the bottom of your .bashrc – this can also go behind an alias, that you must remember to run:

```shell
rm -rf /localrepo/$USER/bin/gemini && export PATH=/apps/android/node-v22.12.0-linux-x64/bin:/localrepo/$USER/bin:$PATH && npm install -g --prefix /localrepo/$USER @google/gemini-cli
echo "GEMINI VERSION BELOW"
/localrepo/$USER/bin/gemini --version
echo "GEMINI VERSION ABOVE"
```

3. Install Google Cloud SDK:  
   1. Local machine: Download and install it in this [link](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install)  
   2. Build server: Use the installation available in the toolchain

```shell
(build server) export PATH=/apps/android/google-cloud-sdk-530.0.0/bin:$PATH
```

4. Authenticate the Google Cloud SDK and use the **moto-gemini-assist** GCP project:  
   1. Remember to grant access to all requested Google permissions

```shell
(local machine) gcloud auth application-default login
```

```shell
(build server) gcloud auth application-default login --no-launch-browser
```

5. Enable telemetry metrics collection:  
   1. **IMPORTANT: this step is mandatory because without telemetry our GCA metrics will be incomplete.**  
   2. GCA uses a configuration file called **settings.json**, if it does not exist, create it in the following folder: **\~/.gemini/** (linux) or **C:\\Users\\\<user\>\\.gemini\\** (windows)  
   3. Add the following content to the file:

```json
{
  "telemetry": {
    "enabled": true,
    "target": "gcp"
  }
}
```

   4. Your file should be similar to the following:

![][image1]

6. Set Google Cloud Project **moto-gemini-assist**:  
   1. **IMPORTANT: this step is also mandatory for telemetry collection and correct Gemini CLI setup**  
   2. Create a **.env** file in **\~/.gemini/** (linux) or **C:\\Users\\\<user\>\\.gemini\\** (windows)  
   3. Add the following content to the file (it is important to add an empty line at the end of the file):

```shell
GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT=moto-gemini-assist
```

7. Sign In with the **Login with Google** option:

```shell
(windows/linux) gemini
```

```shell
(build server) NO_BROWSER=true gemini
```

8. Make sure the project is configured correctly. Run the command **/about** inside Gemini CLI and check if the project is appearing

![][image2]

## IDEs Installation

1. For VSCode, IntelliJ and PyCharm, download the **Gemini Code Assist** plugin/extension (Android Studio has it pre-installed):  
   1. **IntelliJ/PyCharm**: Use **Ctrl+Alt+S** to open the Plugins panel

![][image3]

2. **VSCode**: Use **Ctrl+Shift+X** to open the Extensions panel

![][image4]

2. Click the Gemini Icon and **Sign In** with your Motorola Google account (select **Gemini for Business** if necessary):  
   1. **Android Studio**: Gemini icon on the right side

![][image5]

**IMPORTANT: In Android Studio, if the options below do not appear, try to logout and restart the IDE.**  
![][image6]

2. **IntelliJ/PyCharm**: Gemini icon on the right side

![][image7]

3. **VSCode**: Gemini icon on the left side

![][image8]

3. Select the Google Cloud Project with the active licenses (**moto-gemini-assist**):

**Note**: Please, make sure to set the GCP project **moto-gemini-assist**, this is the project that we have the licenses attached to. This project should only be visible after you **fill the Application Form** and after you **receive a confirmation email** that your license is active.

1. **Android Studio**:

![][image9]  
![][image10]

2. **IntelliJ/PyCharm**:

![][image11]  
![][image12]

3. **VSCode**:

![][image13]  
![][image14]

4. For **VSCode** users, ensure that the Telemetry is enabled in the GCA settings:

![][image15]  
![][image16]

5. Now you are ready to use Gemini Code Assist, feel free to use it.

## Trainings

Check the trainings available in [Gemini Code Assist \- Security Policies and Best Practices](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1kdRPKtnE9K28ryiv0_hlFNWOPUmIaATds7rL3duY6-M/edit?usp=sharing)

## Troubleshooting

This section contains some of the most common issues people face when trying to setup or use Gemini Code Assist in the IDE or Gemini CLI. If you have any issues which are not listed here, create a Jira CR under the AI Tools project with the GCA component: [Create Support CR](https://idart.mot.com/secure/CreateIssue.jspa?pid=43185&issuetype=10)

### Agent Mode not Working

	Make sure to correctly install and configure Gemini CLI either in your local machine or inside the build server. Telemetry configuration is also mandatory.

### Android Studio Outdated

	Please upgrade your Android Studio, older versions do not support Gemini Code Assist.

### Gemini CLI Setup/Authentication

	When setting up Gemini CLI, make sure you follow the guidelines for authentication. You must use the option **“Login with Google”** and must use the environment variable to set the Google Cloud Project **moto-gemini-assist**.  
You can verify if the setup is correct running the **/about** command inside Gemini CLI, and the following information should be set:

* Auth Method: OAuth  
* User Email: your Motorola email address  
* GCP Project: moto-gemini-assist

### Slowness and Crashes

	If you experience slowness or crashes running either the IDE plugin or Gemini CLI, GCA might be trying to scan heavy folders in your project (build outputs, dependencies, etc). In these cases, an additional configuration step is necessary to exclude these files from the AI scanning. Please follow the instructions in the Google documentation: [Exclude files from Gemini Code Assist use](https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini/docs/codeassist/create-aiexclude-file)
